title: CAR VALETOR / CAR CLEANER
description: **WE ARE NOW LOOKING FOR AN ADDITIONAL CAR VALETOR TO JOIN OUR TEAM.** **What The Role Involves** * Washing, hoovering and valeting of customer vehicles in line with Airside Motor Centre standards. * Ensuring all vehicles are processed, cleaned, and presented to our customers in excellent condition. * Inspecting vehicles with salesperson prior to customer handover. * Driving and parking vehicles on the forecourt. * Keeping work areas clean and tidy, organized and safe in accordance with health and safety guidelines. * Assisting in maintaining the dealership and ensuring it is clean and tidy. **Who We Are Looking For** * A hard working and trustworthy person. * Excellent eye for detail. * Good organisational and time keeping skills with the ability to prioritise workload. * Works well as part of a team with the ability to work off your own initiative when necessary. * Good communication skills. * Full driver's license **What's On Offer For The Successful Candidate** * Competitive Salary * Training * Uniform * Bike\-to\-Work scheme **Experience Is Desired But Not Required.** **To apply for this role, click the link below.**

title: Civil Design Engineer
description: Civil Design Engineer, Blanchardstown, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **About The Role** Reporting to the CSA Design Manager, the Civil Design Engineer will be a senior member of the Project Team. You will have notable cross\-discipline design and technical experience in addition to the ability to communicate and engage with stakeholders, at all levels as required. This will include managing design packages from initial review, through to assisting with estimation, procurement, planning and handover for execution on site. With the ability to support the end\-to\-end project deliverables at all stages, the Civil Design Engineer will be integral to the delivery of high valued projects for major data centre clients. You will be highly motivated to succeed in a dynamic, high\-pressure, and fast evolving environment. A strong interest and curiosity in all aspects of the construction industry is expected. The role is responsible for both technical engineering knowledge as well as management of project team deliverables. A key part of the role is the need to prioritise, co\-ordinate and to successfully deliver compliant building facilities. In addition to working as part of a high\-performance team, the Civil Design Engineer will hold responsibility for effectively supporting the CSA Design Manager, as well as MEP Managers and Engineers within Central Engineering, and CSA Managers and Engineers on site, to ensure that relationships are maintained, expectations are met, and Mercury Engineering’s interests are upheld. **Team Management** **Key Responsibilities of the Role:** * Collaborate with architects, engineers, and construction managers to develop comprehensive project plans and ensure delivery on schedule. * Collaborate with any other consultants, subcontractors, supervisors, planners, estimators, quantity surveyors and the general workforce involved in the project. * Liaise with clients and other 3rd parties as required, such as insurance companies, vendors, etc. * Develop comprehensive scope\-of\-works (SoW), task information deliverables list (TIDP), and responsibility matrices (R\&R/ RACI) for civil discipline. * Raise any design\-related risks to planning, commercial and management, and establish a course to minimise their impact. * Maintain accurate records of key design activities (eg. changes, issues) as they arise and escalate to management as appropriate. * Assist the Central Engineering and site teams with proactive contributions to construction and team matters. * Attend design related meetings and provide continual input for optimisation. * Collaborate on new design issues/ changes/ research within the team. * Assist with new tenders and PCSA (pre\-construction services agreements) deliverables within the Business Unit. **Project Management \& Planning** * Oversee the construction process to ensure adherence to design specifications and schedules. * Assist in developing design delivery schedules along with the client, project manager, design team and consultants. * Manage the deliverables and approvals processes of design packages to ensure full approval for procurement. * Check design programmes on projects are aligned to consultant resource and construction/ procurement requirements. * Conduct site visits and report progress and quality of work. * Correctly follow information control and flow standards e.g. for RFIs, use of BIM360, etc. **Technical Engineering** * Ensure civil technical compliance on all projects. * Ensure knowledge of client and country\-specific design standards. * Analyse and interpret geotechnical and SI reports and surveys. * Assess coordination of civil with substructural design. * Assess and mitigate potential risks and issues as they’re identified in design, or that could have potential impact on construction activities. * Review design proposals by others for suitability and constructability. * Offer proposals for added value (VE) and off\-site manufacturing (OSA). * Utilize CAD and BIM software to create detailed civil design proposals for review. * Ensure material choices are aligned to project requirements, budget, and QEHS standards. * Prepare comprehensive handover packs to the site team for any packages under your responsibility. * Coordinate and manage coordination of civil discipline and interfaces with MEP, structure and architecture. * Check plans, drawings, and quantities for accuracy of calculations. * Manage, monitor, and interpret the contract design documents supplied by the client or architect. * Identify requirements for statutory engagements and approvals on projects related to civil networks. **Sustainability** * Integrate sustainable practices into design and construction processes. * Evaluate the environmental impact of projects and propose options for improvements. **Documentation** * Prepare detailed reports, and documentation for project approval and permits. * Maintain accurate records of project progress and changes. * Manage key registers throughout the project design period: + Procurement schedule. + Tech Sub register. + Drawing register. + RFI log. + Deviation tracker. + Lessons Learnt log. + Issues, interface \& scope gap trackers. + Design change tracker. **Other** * Provide technical guidance and support to construction teams. * Review and submit technical submittals. * Assist with recruitment activities for the Central Engineering team as deemed suitable. * Undertake other engineering tasks or duties as required by the CSA Design Manager, Central Engineering Manager and/ or Company. * The Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to consider changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Minimum 5 years post graduate experience in the Industrial, Pharmaceutical, Life Sciences or Data Centre sectors. * In depth knowledge of Statutory Consent Processes, Building Regulations process is essential. * Demonstrable design, technical and construction knowledge is essential for this role. * Experience in leading meetings and coordinating with other team members. * Strong working knowledge of civil design and analysis software such as BIM, Revit, Civil 3D (or equivalent). ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Apply Now

title: Cleaner
description: **About The Role:** As a Cleaner at OCS, you will play a crucial role in providing safe, clean environments in which our customers can thrive in their workspaces. Benefits for working with us: * Free Online Doctor Consultations via Web\-doctor * Competitive Salary * Contributory Pension after qualifying period * 21 Days Annual Leave increasing with service to 26 Days * Additional Leave Day on your Birthday after 1 year service * Staff Benefits/Discount Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Training and Development Opportunities * Free onsite Parking **Your key responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:** * Tidying up work areas, cleaning floors and communal areas. * Vacuuming, sweeping, mopping, emptying bins and other tasks as required. * You may come into regular contact with customers so you must be able to assist them if needed. * You must complete all relevant Health \& Safety records and attend training courses when needed. **The ideal candidate should meet the following criteria:** * You must have Right to Work in the EU * A background in cleaning would be advantageous. * Attention to detail and thoroughness in completing tasks. **About The Company:** OCS UK \& Ireland is a leading facilities management company with 50,000\+ colleagues and a turnover in excess of £2bn. We deliver innovative, award\-winning services within facilities management, hard services, cleaning, security and catering. Our mission is to make people and places the best they can be for our colleagues, customers and the communities we serve. Our commitment to doing business the right way is rooted in our TRUE values \- **Trust, Respect, Unity, and Empowerment.** **Why Work for OCS?** **✅** **Award\-Winning Employer** : Ranked 36th on Glassdoor’s Best Companies to Work For 2025 — we value and motivate our people. **🚀** **Digital Learning** : The OCS Academy offers digital courses and resources to help you build skills and grow your career. 🙂 **Retail Perks** With our Hapi app, you can gain access to exclusive discounts, rewards and wellbeing resources. **📝** **Professional Growth** : 600\+ live learners across UK\&I — Empowering colleagues with further development and qualifications! **💰** **Flexible Pay** : Access a portion of earned wages before payday with our Wagestream App! (Contract Specific) **How to Apply:** If you are interested in joining a business that encourages professional development, career progression and the chance to work with inspirational people then please click apply and register your interest! You can also sign up to our job alerts when registering which will notify you when other suitable vacancies are available. We are an equal opportunities employer and rely on a diverse workforce with a broad range of knowledge, skills, and backgrounds to deliver our goals. We offer an inclusive and welcoming environment and actively encourage applications from all individuals regardless of race, gender, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or age.

title: Cleaner - Quality Control - Afternoon to Evening
description: **Role Overview:** We are recruiting for two flexible mobile support cleaners to strengthen operational coverage across our sites. These roles will primarily focus on covering cleaning shifts, absences, holidays, emergency staffing gaps, and supporting site standards through quality control checks and operational support. Both positions must be flexible to cover morning, daytime, or evening shifts depending on business needs, even though each will have a primary standard schedule. These roles are designed to improve staffing stability, strengthen site performance, and provide better day and evening operational oversight. Available Positions: Position * **Standard shift: 13:30 – 20:00** * **Monday to Friday** * Flexibility required for morning or evening cover depending on operational requirements * Occasional weekend flexibility for special projects or emergency cover * €17 per hour * **Mileage for travel such as annual leap cardKey Responsibilities:** * Cover cleaning shifts across multiple sites * Morning, daytime, or evening flexibility * Emergency and short\-notice cover * Holiday and absence cover * Site visits and quality control checks * Consumables, dispensers, and presentation checks * Reporting issues proactively to management * Photo reporting and site feedback * Supporting operational standards and client satisfaction * Assisting with staffing gaps and special projects Ideal Candidate: * Strong professional cleaning experience essential * Flexible and dependable * Full driving licence strongly preferred * Comfortable travelling between multiple sites * Good English communication and reporting skills * Able to work independently * Strong attention to detail * Supervisory or quality control experience beneficial Please make clear that these are mobile operational support roles, not standard static cleaning positions, and that flexibility, reliability, and willingness to cover multiple sites are essential. Both positions should also be presented as having future progression potential into wider supervisory or operational support roles. Pay: From €17\.00 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * Commercial cleaning: 1 year (required) Language: * Fluent English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Client Optimization Specialist - Bilingual Spanish
description: Our Mission As the world’s number 1 job site\*, our mission is to help people get jobs. We strive to cultivate an inclusive and accessible workplace where all people feel comfortable being themselves. We're looking to grow our teams with more people who share our enthusiasm for innovation and creating the best experience for job seekers. (\*Comscore, Total Visits, March 2025\) Day to Day As a Bilingual (Spanish/English) Client Service Specialist in our Scaled Business Success (SBS) team, you’ll support all clients in our small business segment at different points in their life cycle with Indeed. You’ll assist clients via phone, chat, and email to help them resolve any issues they may have with their accounts or to educate them on how to maximize their accounts effectively to achieve their hiring goals. You will have the opportunity to work with Indeed’s product suite and partner with internal teams to help deliver an outstanding experience on each and every interaction. Responsibilities Have a consultative approach to assess the client’s pain points and suggest a solution approach to “WOW” them. Become a go\-to person for Indeed products across our ever\-growing suite of products. Educate our clients so that they get the most value from Indeed. Be motivated to achieve Client\-centric KPIs such as CSAT and Conversion. Working in a team\-centred environment where continuous learning and experimentation is your day\-to\-day Partner with our Product, Operations, Search Quality, and Aggregation teams to troubleshoot problems and propose solutions. Skills/Competencies Requires a minimum of 2 years of related experience. Fluency in Spanish and English Proven experience in online advertising, search engine marketing (SEM), pay\-per\-click (PPC) advertising, recruitment advertising, or staffing agencies preferred Learn and exhibit tech savviness; your curiosity and passion mean that you can get up to speed and have an impact from day one. Passionate about helping customers find a resolution to their problems Willing to work well under pressure, multitask, and prioritize responsibilities Flexible to work in a changing environment and seek feedback to incorporate it into your work style Motivated to handle a high volume of inbound volume each day influencing positive client outcomes Salary Range Transparency Tier 2 \- Ireland 30,000 \- 46,000 EUR per year Salary Range Disclaimer The base salary range represents the low and high end of the Indeed salary range for this position in the given work location. title: Cobol Developer
description: Sneior COBOL Developer Contract Location: Dublin / Hybrid (1 day per week onsite) My client is looking for an experienced Senior COBOL Developer to support a large\-scale enterprise systems environment. This role would suit someone who is not only strong technically, but also comfortable working closely with business stakeholders, analysing existing systems, defining change impact, and supporting junior developers within the team. The Job You will be responsible for understanding existing legacy systems, gathering and clarifying business requirements, assessing the impact of proposed changes, and working closely with development and testing teams to deliver high\-quality solutions. This is a hands\-on role with a strong leadership element, requiring someone who can take ownership of technical delivery while also mentoring more junior team members. Key Responsibilities * Develop, enhance, and maintain COBOL\-based applications * Analyse existing systems and understand current\-state functionality * Work with business stakeholders to gather and clarify requirements * Assess the scope, impact, and risks of proposed system changes * Collaborate with backend developers, testers, and wider delivery teams * Support solution design and technical decision\-making * Mentor and guide junior developers * Troubleshoot production and testing issues * Ensure code quality, documentation, and delivery standards are maintained Have You? * Strong commercial experience as a COBOL Developer * Experience working in enterprise or large\-scale legacy environments * Ability to understand complex existing systems and business processes * Strong requirements analysis and impact assessment experience * Experience working closely with business users, developers, and testers * Previous lead or mentoring experience * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills * Experience in structured delivery environments * Availability to start around August would be beneficial Nice to Have * Experience in financial services, public sector, insurance, or other regulated environments * Mainframe experience * Experience with JCL, DB2, CICS, IMS, VSAM, or similar technologies

title: Commercial & Finance Coordinator
description: ****Job Description:**** ****Commercial \& Finance Coordinator (Hybrid)**** ****Airbus International Services, Dublin, Ireland**** Airbus is a global leader in aeronautics, space and related services. Airbus offers the most comprehensive range of passenger airliners from 100 to more than 600 seats and business aviation products. Airbus is also a European leader providing tanker, combat, transport and mission aircraft, as well as one of the world’s leading space companies. In helicopters, Airbus provides the most efficient civil and military rotor\-craft solutions worldwide. Airbus International Services Airbus International Services DAC (AIS), is a wholly owned subsidiary of Airbus Helicopters, dedicated to the management of external technical assistance for Airbus clients. With around 100 headcount, our employees are based in jurisdictions across the world, with the management team located in Dublin, Ireland. Join the Commercial team Airbus International Services is a wholly owned subsidiary of Airbus Helicopters, which provides resource based solutions to Airbus customers across the world. Based in Dublin, the Commercial team supports just under 100 globally mobile employees. Roles and Responsibilities Commercial Support: Assist in the preparation, issuance, and tracking of commercial proposals and amendments in line with company pricing policies. Procurement Support: Assist in the posting of GR’s, creation of PO’s and ad hoc tasks . Mission Coordination: Act as the point of contact for mission preparation, including issuing mission numbers, managing travel bookings, and handling expense platforms. Financial Administration: Support the Commercial and Finance Teams with billing, invoice queries, reconciliations, and the management of customer Purchase Orders (POs) . Data Management: Maintain absolute accuracy of commercial and financial data within internal information systems and CRM tools. Operational Liaison: Provide support and assistance to the Commercial Project Managers \& Finance Team on their daily activities. Reporting: Assist in the delivery of monthly pipeline forecasts and cost target monitoring. **Experience** * A third\-level business degree is required * 2\+ years in an office administration role **Skills** * Strong proficiency in Excel and CRM systems; high attention to detail and numerical accuracy * Strong coordination of multiple internal departments and third\-party providers * High level of confidentiality and discretion required * Excellent attention to detail and the ability to spot anomalies * Ability to multitask and prioritise their workload * Excellent communication skills \& ability to work on their own initiative * Problem solving skills * Ability to meet deadlines and take ownership of the role * Team spirit and positive attitude to work are key: proactive attitude * Proficient level in Microsoft Office Suite, in particular Microsoft Excel and Google Suite * SAP experience desirable but not essential Communication: Ability to liaise effectively across multiple internal departments. Organisation: Proven ability to manage multiple deadlines and prioritise daily deliverables in a fast\-paced environment. This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth. ********Company:******** Airbus International Services Designated Activity Company **Employment Type:** Permanent **Experience Level:** Entry Level **Job Family:** Sales, Marketing \& Commercial Contracts By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus. Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief. Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com . At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

title: Commercial Accountant (FMCG)
description: **Commercial Accountant (FMCG)** **Dublin** Armont has partnered with a leading FMCG business seeking a newly / recently qualified accountant to join their high calibre team based in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a commercially minded individual who is interested in a role with month\-end responsibilities, combined with strong exposure to commercial stakeholder partnering. This position is ideal for a newly / recently qualified accountant looking to make their first move into industry from a Big 4 or Top 10 firm. It would also suit a candidate who trained in industry within a similar sector who is seeking the next step in a growing and dynamic business. **The Role** * Responsibility for all aspects of financial and sales reporting * Act as a key finance business partner to the sales team, supporting decision\-making and managing spend * Support the preparation of annual budgets and quarterly forecasts * Prepare and present financial information for monthly and quarterly review meetings * Conduct margin analysis across key product lines * Assist in the supervision and development of junior accounting staff * Month\-end activities, including reconciliations, accruals and variance analysis * Contribute to ad hoc reporting, analysis and project work as required **The Candidate** * Qualified Accountant (0\-2 years’ PQE) * Big 4/Top 10 practice trained or industry experience * Very strong attention to detail * Have good interpersonal \& communication skills * Be self\-motivated, able to plan and prioritise workload in order to achieve deadlines **The Rewards** * Salary range, circa €60,000 to €65,000 * Bonus * Pension * Healthcare * Hybrid working * Parking * Genuine career advancement opportunity

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Polish Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date: August 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give graduates exposure to both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* **Who are we looking for?** * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-orientated and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Polish **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view **What’s in it for you?** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal employee groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1\-1\-1 model **Map Your Future** With our Graduate Program you will have the choice of career paths * You can choose to take a Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * You can choose to take a Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing **Salesforce in Dublin** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Commercial Real Estate Associate
description: **Associate Commercial Real Estate Solicitor** A well managed and large real estate team with consistent streams of interesting work and projects seeks an associate lawyer for a growth based role within their award winning department. You will have trained or practiced post training in a ranked real estate department of a medium, large or boutique firm with at least a partial commercial real estate client base. You will be smart, ambitious and good at problem solving. Ideally you will be self motivated and self accountable and good at managing your time and endeavour. This is a very well paying role as against the market and within the top 5% of firms for reward. You will have moderate and manageable targets and a plethora of industry leaders to learn from.

title: Compliance Support Executive
description: **Job Description:** ==================== **Role Summary** The Compliance Support Executive is a junior level position responsible for the provision of compliance support to RIIL’s Head of Compliance in all aspects of that role including but not limited to regulatory communications, senior management reporting, maintenance of policies and procedures, Anti Money Laundering (AML) and Counter Terrorist Financing (CFT) activities, compliance monitoring and oversight of investment and other regulatory breaches. In addition, the role will assist in monitoring regulatory developments impacting the activities of the Company and Russell Investments’ Irish fund range (the ‘Funds’) and supporting RIIL’s Head of Compliance in the oversight and education of the Company’s delegates (including intra\-group delegates) whose implementation and on\-going compliance with Irish and EU regulations and the Company’s own regulatory policies and procedures is of paramount importance. Working in partnership with RIIL’s Chief Executive Officer and the Director for EMEA Compliance, the role will deputise for RIIL’s Head of Compliance in times of absence. The role requires a high degree of organisation, client focus, communication skills flexibility in managing a varying workload and desire to professionally develop through internal and external studies. This is a 15 month Fixed Term Contract. **The responsibilities of the individual in this position include:** Regulatory \& business conduct * Displaying exemplary conduct and living by our organisation’s Code of Conduct. * Taking personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct across the business. * Effectively and collaboratively identifying, escalating, mitigating and resolving risk, conduct and compliance. Culture * Contributing to maintaining a risk\-aware compliance culture. External regulatory communications * Supporting as required the Company’s engagement with the Central Bank of Ireland and external auditors. This support will be in conjunction with the Company Board, the Company CEO and RIIL’s Head of Compliance. Senior management reporting * Contributing as directed by RIIL’s Head of Compliance to the creation and delivery of comprehensive regulatory reports to the Company’s senior management including: + The Company CEO + The Company Board of directors (as applicable) + Management Committee Regulatory framework * Contributing to the maintenance of the Company’s regulatory framework. * Maintaining regulatory policies, procedures and guidance. * Providing regulatory advice on all day\-to\-day business matters as necessary including advice on new and amended services and products. * Providing regulatory training both planned and ad\-hoc. Regulatory developments * Maintaining awareness of regulatory developments impacting the Company and the Funds and communicating these to others as necessary. * Contributing to impact analysis of such developments. * Providing advice and working alongside others to contribute to implementation activities designed to respond to regulatory developments and particularly interactions with delegates (including intra\-group) to ensure continued compliance by those delegates with the Company and the Fund’s policies and procedures and board direction. Approval and monitoring of delegated activities * Using the results of the regulatory risk assessment and management information from global oversight teams, contributing and supporting the design of oversight programmes of delegates (third\-parties and intra\-group) both in terms of initial on\-boarding and on\-going oversight activities. Being involved in the formal approval of delegate hires. * Working with other functions to ensure robustness of the Company’s governance arrangements relating to outsourcing and to ensure that for each outsource service provider, the arrangements meet the European Union and the Central Bank of Ireland’s outsourcing rules and guidance. * Supporting the conduct of monitoring and oversight reviews (including desk\-based and on\-site reviews) of delegates (third\-parties and intra\-group). * On\-site may involve travelling both in\-state and international. * Recording outcomes of oversight reviews and presenting findings in accordance with senior management reporting requirements. AML/CTF * Supporting RIIL’s Head of Compliance/ MLRO for the Funds \& the Company incorporating: + Evaluation of suspicious transaction reports; onward reporting to relevant authorities where necessary. + Maintenance of AML/CTF risk assessment, policies and procedures for the Funds \& the Company in line with Irish regulation and guidance. + Oversight of Irish domiciled fund administrators. + Creation and roll out of AML/CTF training as necessary. + Point of escalation for fund administrators and Client Service/Sales teams. + Monthly transaction surveillance \& stopped accounts monitoring. + Quarterly reporting to governance committee \& Board(s). * Liaison with AML Officers in RIIL branch offices as appropriate. * Liaison with EMEA MLRO on issues that may impact other EMEA group companies. * Maintenance of fund beneficial ownership registers. Compliance Monitoring * Using the results of the regulatory risk assessment to contribute to the design of the EMEA compliance monitoring programme. * Supporting the performance of compliance monitoring reviews and coordinating reviews with other EMEA and Global compliance personnel. * Working with intra\-group teams to ensure appropriate controls are in place in relation to trading within the RIIL client accounts. Compliant Oversight * Supporting the RIIL Head of Compliance as the complaint management function. * Overseeing the handling of client complaints ensuring fair treatment. **The successful candidate will have extensive demonstrable skills and experiences including the following:** * Financial or business\-related degree or relevant prior experience from working in a similar role. * Basic understanding of the financial services industry in Ireland and how that industry is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland but also other EU authorities (i.e. ESMA). * Previously carried out a similar role within an investment firm, fund management company or fund administrator. * Knowledge of MiFID \& AML (\& related guidance) preferrable. * Consistent and uncompromising adherence to strong moral and ethical principles and values * Good communication, listening, speaking and writing skills delivered to audiences at all levels of seniority. * Ability to act with diplomacy and present information with clarity to associates, clients and to service providers. * An entry level awareness of financial products and services offered by RIIL but also the wider Russell Investments Group including, who offers them and how different functional teams interact. * Ability to assist the growth of RIIL and the group whilst being conscious of operational commitments, current priorities and emerging opportunities. * Self\-motivated, strength to work under pressure, make difficult decisions and stand by them, escalating as appropriate. * Good teamwork skills, with the ability to organise and prioritise work. * Ability to investigate operational issues, determine how new requirements need to be implemented and look for opportunities to improve service quality, efficiency, and the controls environment.
