title: Lawyer - Projects and Construction
description: **Title: Senior Associate** **Department: Projects and Construction** William Fry is seeking a dedicated and ambitious senior lawyer to join our dynamic Projects \& Construction team at our Dublin office. This team provides expert advice on a wide range of construction, energy, infrastructure, and procurement matters. In this role, you will engage in a variety of complex transactions across diverse sectors including commercial development, residential projects, infrastructure, transport, and energy. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with other departments, such as Real Estate, Banking, and Corporate, on construction\-related aspects of transactions, disputes, development projects, financing, refinancing, and M\&A transactions. This is an exciting opportunity to join a high\-performing, and supportive team and work with a broad client base while contributing to the firm's ongoing success. **The Role** In this role you will be responsible for * Providing advice on a variety of construction\-related legal issues, including project procurement, disputes, project management, mid\-stream issues like extensions of time (EOT) and claims, negotiation of construction contracts, and agreements related to project financing and development, as well as construction elements in M\&A transactions. * Drafting and negotiating key contracts and agreements for diverse project types. * Participating in some of Ireland's largest and most complex construction projects, engaging with large Irish and global clients, including developers, contractors, and funders. **The Person** * You have a minimum of 5 years post\-qualification experience (PQE) within a reputable law firm or in\-house legal team, with a focus on construction, energy infrastructure, and/or other major projects. * You are proficient in drafting and negotiating construction contracts (especially RIAI, NEC, and professional appointments) and related documents, with a solid understanding of market requirements. * You possess strong interpersonal skills and have the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * You are client focused and have a proven track record of building and maintaining strong client relationships. * You are interested in mentoring and developing junior lawyers on the team. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: Manufacturing Technician

description: **About Nap OS Workforce \| Napblog Limited** Nap OS Workforce is the talent delivery engine of Napblog Limited — Ireland's Innovation Delivery Company. Our ecosystem bridges the gap between talent and industry through structured project delivery, program management, and skills development. **Role Overview** We are looking for a motivated and curious Research Intern to join the Nap OS Workforce team. This is a hands\-on internship where you will support live R\&D and innovation projects, conduct market and industry research, and contribute to the delivery of real commercial outcomes for Irish companies. You will work under the guidance of a dedicated Program Manager and gain verified, portfolio\-ready work experience. **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct desk research, competitor analysis, and industry landscape reviews to support active client projects * Gather, synthesise, and present data findings in clear written reports and presentations * Support the Napblog PM team with project scoping, documentation, and milestone tracking * Assist in mapping innovation challenges to Enterprise Ireland funding pathways (Innovation Vouchers, R\&D Tax Credits) * Contribute to content, case studies, and knowledge assets for the Nap OS platform * Participate in weekly feedback cycles and 1:1 sessions with your Program Manager * Collaborate with cross\-functional team members on live project deliverables **What You Will Gain** * Verified work experience credentialled by Napblog Limited * A portfolio of real commercial research deliverables * LinkedIn and resume\-ready project evidence * Exposure to Enterprise Ireland's R\&D innovation ecosystem * Mentorship from experienced Program Managers * Dedicated work email and Slack access * A Work Experience Certificate upon successful completion **Who We Are Looking For** * A recent graduate, final\-year student, or career changer with a strong interest in research, innovation, or business * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work independently and manage tasks in a remote environment * Comfortable synthesising information from multiple sources into clear, structured outputs * Genuine interest in Ireland's startup and R\&D ecosystem * Familiarity with Google Suite, Notion, or similar productivity tools is a plus **Details** * Location: Remote (Ireland\-based preferred) * Duration: 6 months (Floating Contract) * Type: Internship * Platform: All work delivered via Nap OS Workforce Apply now and start building the work experience that gets you hired. Learn more: https://napblog.com \& Product: Nap OS at os.napblog.com

title: Network Operations Engineer, Optical Transport
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in a technical field or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience in Optical or IP network operations. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience with Unix/Linux administration, with scripting/coding. * Ability to work weekend days and shifts outside of normal business hours (typically 4\-day weeks, with no off\-shift pager duty). * Ability to communicate in English fluently to support client relationship management in this region. * Excellent troubleshooting and creative problem\-solving skills. **About The Job** As a Network Implementation Engineer, you will be the initial point of our efforts to execute deployment, maintenance, and operations of private data networks worldwide. You will work with Technical Program Managers, Network Engineers, Design and Infrastructure Engineers, Field Engineers within Google, as well as construction and telecommunications vendors and contractors, all to position your team and organization for success. You will facilitate faster, better, and more efficient, positive outcomes for the business and our customers. Your objective will be to build the world’s most reliable, cost\-effective and scalable network to support all of our current and future customers and users globally. Google's network provides services to millions of Internet users around the world. Our metros are on the edge of our network where Google connects to its users. The Network Team is responsible for operating that network reliably and at scale. Our team owns the full life cycle of all space, power, and network assets in all of Google’s data centers and metro points of presence globally. From the foundation, we are involved from site acquisition to construction and are accountable for what space and power is delivered. We're involved in every facet of network delivery from architecture and design to installation, configuration, activation, and commissioning. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €76000 \- €78000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Monitor network stability and performance, ensuring 24x7 operations and resolving service impacts as they occur. * Support Google's internal teams by troubleshooting service impacting issues involving the network. * Act as an externally facing point of contact to facilitate handling of problem reports and maintain relations with network peers and vendors. * Act as an internally facing point of contact to escalate technical issues and communicate network status. * Oversee and execute planned network maintenance work, minimizing impact to Google services. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: ONSITE SUPPORT TECHNICIAN
description: **HCLTECH** HCLTech is a global technology company, spread across 60 countries, delivering industry\-leading capabilities centered around digital, engineering, cloud and AI, powered by a broad portfolio of technology services and products. We work with clients across all major verticals, providing industry solutions for Financial Services, Manufacturing, Life Sciences and Healthcare, Technology and Services, Telecom and Media, Retail and CPG, and Public Services. We re powered by our people a global, diverse, multi\-generational talent \- representing 161 nationalities whose unique spark, perspective and boundless passion drive our culture of proactive value creation and problem\-solving. Our purpose is to bring together the best of technology and our people to supercharge progress for everyone, everywhere our clients, partners, their stakeholders, communities, and the planet. As a company, we are deeply focused on accelerating our ESG agenda. We are also creating technology\-enabled sustainable solutions with and for our clients and partners. We embed ESG imperatives into every aspect of our business and ensure that the progress we supercharge is responsible, inclusive and beneficial to all our stakeholders in the long term. We have committed to achieving net zero by 2040\. To learn more about how we can supercharge progress for you, visit www.hcltech.com ***Note: Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland.*** ***Fluent in English*** **Job Summary:** As an IT Field Services \& Onsite Support Technician, you will be a key member of our team, providing essential on\-site technical support to our clients. You will be the first point of contact for resolving their technology issues, ensuring smooth operations, and maximizing their productivity. You will play a crucial role in maintaining the IT infrastructure and ensuring satisfaction. You will troubleshoot, diagnose, and resolve hardware and software issues, ensuring optimal system performance and user satisfaction. This role requires strong technical skills, excellent problem\-solving abilities, and a customer\-centric approach ***Job Role and Responsibilities:*** **Key Responsibilities:** **On\-Site Support:** * Travel to client sites to provide in\-person technical assistance. * Install, configure, and upgrade computer hardware and software. * Troubleshoot and resolve a wide range of IT issues, including network connectivity, printer problems, and software malfunctions. * Audio and Video equipment Support including Health Checks * Asset Management \& Disposal * Data recovery and backup solutions **Problem Diagnosis and Resolution:** * Utilize diagnostic tools and techniques to identify the root cause of technical problems. * Implement effective troubleshooting strategies to resolve issues promptly and efficiently. * Escalate complex issues to higher\-level support teams as needed. * Image and install software on Windows, and Apple laptops/desktops including IMAC (installations, moves, additions, and changes) and Remote support. * Coordination with IT Network and Unified Communications team and dispatch vendor technicians to install network equipment, coordinate with Client’s third\-party suppliers for issues/repair of circuits and RMA. * Eyes, hands, and feet support for network issues including setup, installation and supporting the configuration as needed, testing circuits, connectivity, and validation of network equipment. * Coordinate with external vendors for dispatch support. **Asset Management:** * Inventory and Asset Management – Depot Operations * Ensure proper handling and disposal of IT assets. * Shipping coordination, Disposal, redeployment, and Asset reclaim including the equipment in Telco rooms. * Hardware Refresh, Depot Services support, Warranty and Out\-of\-warranty repair and replacement coordination **Network Management** * Streamline network operations by coordinating with ITNUC teams to schedule efficient installations. * Proactively address circuit issues and facilitate repairs in partnership with clients' third\-party suppliers to minimize downtime. * Implement and maintain meticulous cable management practices to ensure optimal network performance and aesthetics. **Customer Service:** * Interact with clients in a professional and courteous manner. * Clearly communicate technical information to non\-technical users. * Document all service requests, resolutions, and client interactions. * Executive (VIP) Support – Work on the issue/troubleshooting remotely with diligence. **Ticket Queue Management** * Act and update the tickets timely with clear information to avoid ticket escalations. * Daily follow up on the tickets pending for customers or vendors. * Document the troubleshooting steps followed, reasons for pending or transferring to another queue clearly. * Resolve tickets within the SLA, with confirmed resolution to avoid any tickets getting reopened. * Continued focus on keeping the backlog of tickets low and achieve higher productivity (on average 4 or more tickets per day). **Qualifications:** **An ideal candidate to have:** * 3\-5 years of hands\-on experience providing IT support in deskside, remote desktop, or field environments. * Proficient in troubleshooting and resolving issues on Windows and macOS devices, including laptops and desktops. * Skilled in efficient Windows OS imaging processes. * Proficiency in troubleshooting network connectivity issues. * Knowledge of networking protocols, such as TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, and routing protocols. * Technical Certifications * Prior experience in Telcom industry as Field Services Technician will be an advantage. **Hardware Expertise:** * **Computer Assembly and Repair:** Building, upgrading, and repairing desktops, laptops, and servers. * **Peripheral Device Installation:** Setting up printers, scanners, external hard drives, and other devices. * **Hardware Troubleshooting:** Diagnosing and resolving issues with hardware components like motherboards, CPUs, RAM, and power supplies. * **Mobile Device Repair:** Fixing cracked screens, battery replacements, and other hardware issues for smartphones and tablets – good to have skill. * **Audio Video Equipment:** Basic understanding of AV equipment connections. **Software Proficiency** * **Operating System Installation and Configuration:** Installing and configuring Windows, macOS, and various Linux distributions. * **Software Installation and Troubleshooting** * **Driver Installation and Updates:** Installing and updating device drivers to ensure optimal performance. * **Data Recovery:** Recovering lost data from damaged hard drives, SSDs, and other storage devices. **Networking Knowledge:** * **Network Setup and Configuration:** Configure network devices, including routers, switches, and Wi\-Fi access points. * **Network Troubleshooting:** Diagnosing and resolving network connectivity issues, such as slow internet speeds, Wi\-Fi problems, and network security threats. * **Cable Management:** Organize and label cables to ensure efficient airflow and accessibility. **Customer Service Skills:** * **Effective Communication:** Clearly explaining technical concepts to non\-technical customers. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * **Problem\-Solving:** Identifying and resolving customer issues efficiently and accurately. * **Patience and Empathy:** Ability to build professional rapport with clients and maintain positive relationships. * Attention to detail and strong organizational skills. **Time Management:** * Efficient time management to meet deadlines and optimize work schedules. **Additional Requirements:** * Valid driver's license and reliable transportation. * Ability to lift and carry heavy equipment. * Ability to use ladder for troubleshooting access points and cabling. * Willingness to work flexible hours, including occasional evenings, weekends, and holidays. * Up to 30 \-40% country wide travel. **Benefits** * A supportive, diverse, and global team with a brilliant culture. * Competitive compensation and benefits that includes up to 20 days’ vacation per year, various insurances like Term life and Business Travel insurance. These are apart from the statutory benefits applicable in the country. Employee benefits are regulated by an internal policy that contains full details regarding the entitlement and conditions for the benefits as per the law of the land. * Great opportunities to make the role your own, upskill yourself and get involved with exciting projects. * Total Wellbeing is our focus. Alongside your professional excellence, you join the likeminded colleagues to create a larger impact within the company and society at large in your chosen area of passion \- CSR Council, Diversity Council, Women Connect, Sparks – Engagement Champion to name a few. For more information on how we process your personal data, please refer to HCLTech’s **Candidate Data Privacy Notice .** To know more about us visit – www.hcltech.com

title: PMO & Automation Analyst - Technology (IT PMO)
description: **Join us** At Vodafone, we’re not just shaping the future of connectivity for our customers — we’re shaping the future for everyone who joins our team. When you work with us, you’re part of a global mission to connect people, solve complex challenges, and create a sustainable and more inclusive world. If you want to grow your career whilst finding the right balance between work and life, Vodafone offers the opportunity to help you belong and make a real impact. **Role Summary** As a **PMO \& Automation Analyst** , you will lead the transformation of project governance processes through automation and data\-driven insights. You will work closely with Project Managers, Finance, and our offshore VOIS teams to implement AI\-driven workflows, Power Automate solutions, and interactive dashboards that streamline project financial management and reporting. Your role is to reduce manual effort and improve efficiency by replacing traditional administrative tasks with smart, automated processes. This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, tech\-savvy PMO professional with a passion for innovation, data, and continuous improvement within a dynamic enterprise environment. **What You Will Do** **Key Responsibilities:\-** **Design \& Manage Automated Workflows:** Develop and maintain automated processes (using tools like Power Automate and other AI/ML technologies) for key project governance activities, such as resource time tracking, approvals, and financial updates – ensuring these workflows run smoothly and minimize manual intervention. **Financial Governance \& Budget Tracking:** Partner with Project Managers and Finance to maintain accurate, real\-time project budgets and forecasts. Use digital tools and dashboards to track spend, forecast costs, and ensure resources are allocated efficiently. **Offshore (VOIS) Budget Coordination:** Coordinate with offshore/VOIS teams to manage and track budgets for outsourced project components. Ensure seamless integration of offshore costs into overall project financials and that all expenses are properly approved and documented. **Dynamic Reporting \& Dashboards:** Create and refine interactive dashboards and management reports (e.g., in Power BI or similar tools) that give senior stakeholders clear, real\-time visibility of project status, risks, and key metrics. Continuously improve these reporting outputs using feedback and new data techniques. **Advanced Analytics \& Insights:** Perform ad\-hoc data analysis on project and portfolio data to identify trends, anomalies, or improvement opportunities. Present insights through high\-quality visuals and presentations (e.g., slides, charts) to support decision\-making by leadership. **Governance \& Standards Ownership:** Drive consistency and best practices across the project management community by developing and promoting standard templates, automated processes, and data governance standards. Ensure that project teams adhere to these standards for efficient, aligned ways of working. **Continuous Improvement \& Innovation:** Proactively identify process inefficiencies or manual work steps in project and financial management. Conceive and implement improvements – from small process tweaks to larger automation initiatives – that increase productivity and reduce errors. **Stakeholder Engagement \& Training:** Act as a champion for new tools and processes, coaching and supporting project teams in adopting automated solutions. Provide training or guidance to colleagues on using new dashboards and workflow tools, building a culture of data\-driven decision making. **What You’ll Need** **Skills \& Experience:** **Technical Proficiency:** Hands\-on experience with (or strong aptitude to learn) automation and analytics tools such as Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, or similar workflow/BI platforms. Ability to design simple automation scripts or use AI\-based tools to streamline processes. **Financial \& Analytical Skills:** Solid understanding of project budgeting, forecasting, and cost management principles. Comfortable working with financial data and large datasets, demonstrating strong analysis and numeracy skills with high attention to detail and accuracy. **Project \& Tool Knowledge:** Familiarity with project management processes and common tools (e.g., SharePoint, Microsoft Project or similar PM software). Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite, especially Excel for data manipulation and PowerPoint for presentation of complex information. **Problem\-Solving \& Innovation:** A proactive, curious mindset with proven ability to identify process gaps and implement creative technical solutions. Willingness to continuously learn and experiment with new technologies (AI, bots, data analytics) to improve ways of working. **Self\-Management \& Collaboration:** Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities to deliver high\-quality outputs with minimal supervision. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills – able to work effectively with cross\-functional teams, explain technical concepts in simple terms, and influence stakeholders to embrace new processes. **Desirable** **PMO/Project Experience:** Previous experience in a Project Management Office, IT project delivery, or digital transformation support role, especially within a large or complex organisation. **Automation Credentials:** Exposure to robotic process automation (RPA), simple scripting (e.g., VBA, Python), or AI\-driven applications in a business context. Any relevant certification in Microsoft Power Platform or data analytics would be an advantage. **Global/Offshore Collaboration:** Experience working with offshore or remote teams and an understanding of cultural dynamics in a global organisation. **Industry Knowledge:** Familiarity with telecommunications or technology projects and understanding of their common challenges. **What We’re Looking For:** **A Tech\-Enthusiastic Problem Solver:** A motivated, detail\-oriented individual who gets excited about leveraging technology and data to drive efficiency and improve processes. **A Collaborative Influencer:** Someone who enjoys engaging with diverse stakeholders (from Project Managers to senior leaders and offshore colleagues) and can influence others to adopt new tools and practices through clear communication and demonstration of value. **A Change Champion:** A team player who embraces innovation and values governance, balancing consistency with continuous improvement. You are not afraid to challenge the status quo and can navigate change positively, acting as a champion for new ways of working. **A Growth Mindset:** An individual keen to develop their skills in automation, data analysis, and project governance within a large, dynamic enterprise. You thrive on learning new technologies and have the adaptability to evolve with the role as the organisation continues its digital transformation. **Not a perfect fit?** Worried that you don’t meet all the desired criteria exactly? At Vodafone we are passionate about empowering people and creating a workplace where everyone can thrive, whatever their personal or professional background. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align exactly with every part of the job description, we encourage you to still apply — you may be the right candidate for this role or another opportunity. **What’s in it for you?** * The flexibility to work how you perform best, with hybrid ways of working and real focus on work\-life balance * A competitive package including salary, bonus and a strong pension to support you now and long\-term * ''Generous'' flexible benefits allowance, plus access to comprehensive health and wellbeing support * Access to our on\-site wellness centre and gym, alongside a wider wellbeing programme * Free mobile plan, device offers and exclusive discounts for you, your family and friends * Market\-leading family policies and flexibility to support you at every stage of life * Real opportunities for career growth through learning, development and internal progression **Who We Are** We are a leading international Telco, serving millions of customers. At Vodafone, we believe that connectivity is a force for good. If we use it for the things that really matter, it can improve people’s lives and the world around us. Through our technology, we empower people — connecting everyone regardless of who they are or where they live — and we protect the planet, whilst helping our customers do the same. Belonging at Vodafone isn’t a concept; it’s lived, breathed, and cultivated through everything we do. You’ll be part of a global and diverse community with many different minds, abilities, backgrounds and cultures. We’re committed to increasing diversity, ensuring equal representation, and making Vodafone a place where everyone feels safe, valued and included. Vodafone is committed to supporting candidates with disabilities during the recruitment process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know. **Together we can.**

title: PMO Analyst - EPOS - 12 Month FTC
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way **Technology at Primark** Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. We are seeking a highly capable PMO Analyst to support the Programme Management Office (PMO) function for a large\-scale EPOS transformation across a global fast fashion retail business. This individual will be responsible for maintaining robust programme controls, reporting frameworks, resource governance, and delivery standards across the programme lifecycle. The ideal candidate will bring senior PMO experience from complex, multi\-workstream technology or retail transformation programmes. * Lead the day\-to\-day operation of the PMO function across the EPOS transformation * Enforce programme governance standards, processes, and reporting cycles adhering to Primark policies and Delivery Governance Framework * Own the programme’s RAID (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) framework and ensure effective tracking and escalation * Oversee integrated planning, milestone tracking, resource forecasting, and dependency management * Manage programme reporting to steering groups, exec stakeholders, and senior leadership * Support programme/quality assurance across all delivery activities, including stage gates and audit readiness * Proven 5\+ years minimum experience supporting PMO functions in large\-scale, complex programmes (ideally retail or EPOS\-related) * Exceptional skills in governance, planning, RAID management, and stakeholder reporting * Experience in multi\-vendor, multi\-geography delivery environments * Advanced skills in Microsoft Project, Excel, PowerPoint, and project portfolio tools (e.g. Planisware, Clarity, or similar) * Strong interpersonal, facilitation, and communication skills at all levels **Desirable:** * PRINCE2, MSP, P3O or similar certifications * Background in technology or retail transformation **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-10476

title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines **Benefits:** * Staff Discount * Employee Assistance Programme * Day time hours Monday \- Friday 9am\-6pm * Cycle to Work Scheme * Pension Scheme

title: People Services Specialist
description: Slalom is a global, human\-centric business and technology consulting firm. We specialise in partnering with organisations that aspire to excellence, helping them tackle complex challenges and achieve transformative results through strategy, technology, and business transformation services. By prioritising people, Slalom creates a unique consulting experience, with a team of strategists and engineers delivering practical, end\-to\-end solutions that drive impactful outcomes for our clients. We empower people and organisations to dream bigger, move faster, and build better tomorrows for all. We are seeking an experienced and motivated People Services Specialist to join our EMEA regional central HR support team. This is a hands\-on role where you are part of a team responsible for providing HR related administrative and analytical support to team members in EMEA (Germany, Ireland, Netherlands and UK). **What You’ll Do:** * Serve as the primary HR contact for employees in EMEA, providing guidance on policies, benefits, and HR processes. * Process daily HR transactions and queries, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely resolution, including onboarding, offboarding, and employment documentation in accordance with country\-specific labor laws. * Administer leave of absence programs and time\-off policies in line with local legislation, and collaborate with Payroll for accurate employee data processing. * Manage reports, compliance documentation, and conduct audits and reconciliations of employee data as required by local regulations. * Support special HR projects, including planning, communication, training, and other activities that enhance team effectiveness across the EMEA region. **What we are looking for:** * 2\-3 years of experience in HR operations or shared services, ideally within a global matrix organization * Excellent verbal and written communication skills with an ability to work discretely on a wide range of sensitive and confidential issues * Strong stakeholder management and adaptability in a fast\-paced, multicultural environment. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office; experience with Workday and ServiceNow is a plus. * Attention to detail with a passion for excellent customer service; resourceful with a natural curiosity * Work with a sense of urgency without sacrificing quality or accuracy; ability to thrive in an ambiguous and rapidly changing environment **Why Join Us?** Join a collaborative, client\-focused team where your expertise will directly impact delivery quality and customer success. Leverage your business analysis and quality assurance skills to drive innovation and help clients maximize the value of their technology investments. We have a question for you \- and it’s something we’re passionate about. Can you imagine a world in which you can truly love your life and your work? Well, we have some good news \- creating that world and making this vision a reality is what we get out of bed for; it’s our north star. But what do we really mean when we talk about loving your life and your work? Let’s look at the ways in which we help our team members to achieve this \- and the ‘how’. Deep connections, better outcome \- We have deep relationships with leading technology partners and they love us for our innovative and outcome based approach. Our people are passionate about solving our clients problems using the tech that’s the best solution for them. What’s more, we’re there to work side\-by\-side with our client teams to enable them for success long after we’ve gone. We’re all about momentum that outlasts us. Flexibility \- Life is busy, and we understand that. Our team often juggles work, family, personal commitments, and crucial client obligations. We prioritise supporting our people in balancing what matters to them while ensuring we meet our client commitments. Flexibility is key, as we sometimes need to adapt to meet client needs. This way, our team can work on high\-impact projects they’ll love, knowing they have the flexibility to manage their personal and professional lives effectively. People\-first \- Great solutions start with great people. At our employee\-led company, your voice matters, and we love to hear your feedback. By leading with kindness, empathy, and striving for equity, we create better experiences for everyone. Our culture encourages passion, adventure, adaptability, and diverse thinking. Inclusion, diversity, and equity are our top priorities, empowering our team to be their best selves. Together, we can create a great future by working collaboratively and supporting each other. Rewards \-There’s no shying away from it \- the compensation and benefits on offer have to be competitive too, right? We know that. That’s why we have a dedicated team working with our leaders to ensure our packages are fair, competitive, and rewarding! Who are we... We are Slalom! Slalom is a next\-generation professional services company creating value at the intersection of business, technology, and humanity. With our fiercely human approach, we deeply understand our customers—and their customers—to deliver practical, end\-to\-end solutions that drive meaningful impact. Supported by over 700 technology partners, our 10,000\+ team members in 11 countries and 52 offices we Inspire people and organisations to envision greater possibilities, accelerate progress, and shape a brighter future for everyone. We’re honoured to be consistently recognised as a great place to work, including being one of Fortune’s 100 Best Companies to Work for nine years running. Read more at \[1] slalom.com. Want to learn more? Why not apply?? We would love to hear from you! Slalom prides itself on helping our team members thrive in their work and life. As a result, Slalom is proud to invest in our team members with competitive and innovative benefit programs and services that meet the unique needs of our diverse workforce. If you require any assistance with regards to reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please do not hesitate to contact us \- we will always be happy to help.

title: Personal Injury Claims Handler
description: Are you a skilled claims professional looking to elevate your career within a dynamic, forward\-thinking team? 360 Search is delighted to represent a leading player in the Irish insurance claims sector as they embark on an exciting phase of strategic expansion. Driven by market shifts and prestigious new insurer partnerships, this organisation is growing its personal injury team in Dublin. As a Personal Injury Claims Handler, you will play a pivotal role in managing a diverse portfolio of injury\-related claims stemming from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases. Working within a collaborative, hybrid environment, you will take ownership of the full claim lifecycle, ensuring fair settlements while helping to mentor junior colleagues in a supportive, professional atmosphere. Key Responsibilities * **End\-to\-End Portfolio Management:** Manage a diverse portfolio of personal injury claims originating from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases from initial notification through to final settlement. * **Litigation \& Settlement Negotiation:** Proactively negotiate settlements and manage litigation processes, ensuring optimal, cost\-effective outcomes in line with Irish civil liability frameworks. * **MGA \& Insurer Collaboration:** Act as a key liaison between MGAs and insurers, maintaining strong stakeholder relationships and utilising various industry databases and systems to ensure seamless reporting. * **Efficient Cycle Management:** Drive claims progression efficiently, aiming to maintain an average claim lifecycle of 60 days, with damage settlements targeted within the 28\-to\-30\-day range. * **Mentorship \& Support:** Share your expertise to support and mentor junior team members, helping to foster a high\-performance culture focused on continuous development. Required Skills \& Experience * **Irish Claims Expertise:** You must possess a minimum of 3 years of hands\-on experience in personal injury claims handling specifically within the Irish market. * **Litigation \& Negotiation Skills:** Strong practical knowledge of the Irish litigation process and a proven track record of successful settlement negotiations. * **Regulatory \& System Familiarity:** Sound understanding of key insurance databases, industry systems, and Irish regulatory compliance standards. * **Communication \& Collaboration:** Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build trust with claimants, insurers, and legal professionals. * **Dublin\-Based / Hybrid Ready:** Located in or around Dublin, with a desire to work in a hybrid setup (please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this permanent, full\-time position). Nice\-to\-Have * **High\-Volume Claims Management:** Experience managing high\-volume claims portfolios (ideally carrying 150 to 200 active claims per handler). * **Industry Qualifications:** Progress towards or completion of relevant CIP, MDI, or ACII qualifications. What We Offer / Why Join By joining 360 Search’s client on a permanent, full\-time basis, you will benefit from a highly competitive base salary and a comprehensive rewards package. You will enjoy a modern hybrid working structure, with the potential for fully remote working arrangements for the right candidate. Beyond the day\-to\-day, this role offers genuine, structured career progression opportunities within a growing business that values mentorship, professional development, and a healthy work\-life balance.

Driven by market shifts and prestigious new insurer partnerships, this organisation is growing its personal injury team in Dublin. As a Personal Injury Claims Handler, you will play a pivotal role in managing a diverse portfolio of injury\-related claims stemming from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases. Working within a collaborative, hybrid environment, you will take ownership of the full claim lifecycle, ensuring fair settlements while helping to mentor junior colleagues in a supportive, professional atmosphere. Key Responsibilities * **End\-to\-End Portfolio Management:** Manage a diverse portfolio of personal injury claims originating from motor, fleet, private client, and public liability cases from initial notification through to final settlement. * **Litigation \& Settlement Negotiation:** Proactively negotiate settlements and manage litigation processes, ensuring optimal, cost\-effective outcomes in line with Irish civil liability frameworks. * **MGA \& Insurer Collaboration:** Act as a key liaison between MGAs and insurers, maintaining strong stakeholder relationships and utilising various industry databases and systems to ensure seamless reporting. * **Efficient Cycle Management:** Drive claims progression efficiently, aiming to maintain an average claim lifecycle of 60 days, with damage settlements targeted within the 28\-to\-30\-day range. * **Mentorship \& Support:** Share your expertise to support and mentor junior team members, helping to foster a high\-performance culture focused on continuous development. Required Skills \& Experience * **Irish Claims Expertise:** You must possess a minimum of 3 years of hands\-on experience in personal injury claims handling specifically within the Irish market. * **Litigation \& Negotiation Skills:** Strong practical knowledge of the Irish litigation process and a proven track record of successful settlement negotiations. * **Regulatory \& System Familiarity:** Sound understanding of key insurance databases, industry systems, and Irish regulatory compliance standards. * **Communication \& Collaboration:** Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build trust with claimants, insurers, and legal professionals. * **Dublin\-Based / Hybrid Ready:** Located in or around Dublin, with a desire to work in a hybrid setup (please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this permanent, full\-time position). Nice\-to\-Have * **High\-Volume Claims Management:** Experience managing high\-volume claims portfolios (ideally carrying 150 to 200 active claims per handler). * **Industry Qualifications:** Progress towards or completion of relevant CIP, MDI, or ACII qualifications. What We Offer / Why Join By joining 360 Search’s client on a permanent, full\-time basis, you will benefit from a highly competitive base salary and a comprehensive rewards package. You will enjoy a modern hybrid working structure, with the potential for fully remote working arrangements for the right candidate. Beyond the day\-to\-day, this role offers genuine, structured career progression opportunities within a growing business that values mentorship, professional development, and a healthy work\-life balance.

title: Planner
description: Planner, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * Responsible for a portfolio of tender projects * Reporting to Planning Department * Tender Schedule Development * Prepare comprehensive tender schedules (Level 2 to 4\), schedule narratives, and progress curves aligned with bid requirements. * Ensure programmes align with Schedule Specifications, Employer Requirements, contract conditions and Internal SOP/Guidelines. * Assist with the creation of 4D models and / or 2D phasing plans to present construction sequence, logistics and phasing strategies. * Collaborate with Internal teams to clarify milestones, scope, sequencing and construction methodology. * Planning \& Scheduling * Develop tender programmes across all phases including design, procurement, preconstruction, construction, commissioning and handover phases. Perform delay analysis, float path reviews, and milestone projections. * Data Management \& Reporting * Maintain planning databases and produce progress reports for internal and client review. * Provide accurate data for payment applications and contract reviews. * Stakeholder Engagement * Act as the primary point of contact for tender planning queries. Liaise with clients, subcontractors, and internal teams to ensure alignment by assisting in schedule workshop meetings. * Attend mid\-bid meetings to present Tender Schedule and deliverables as required. * Resource \& Cost Loading * Apply resource and cash flow loading to schedules in coordination with estimators. * Leadership \& Training * Mentor junior planners and promote best practices in planning processes. Provide cover for Planners responsibilities when required. * Compliance \& Governance * Ensure adherence to company SOPs and client standards for all planning deliverables. * Making relevant parties aware of issues and advising on solutions * Training staff if required * Covering Senior Tender Planner responsibilities when required * All other duties associated with role **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Ability to carry out the roles and responsibilities as defined above * Background in construction\-related degree or trade * 5\+ years’ experience in Project Planning of large construction projects * Excellent communication skills * Excellent IT and software skills (including Oracle P6 and Excel) * Proven ability to work effectively in a team * Proven ability to work within strict deadlines * Knowledge of and commitment to Health \& Safety ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Power Platform Developer
description: Our Dublin\-based client is looking for a Power Platform Developer to join their team. They are seeking a developer who can enhance and expand their digital solutions landscape through the design, development, and support of Microsoft Power Platform applications, integrating with Microsoft 365 technologies including SharePoint and Dynamics 365\. This position is with a well\-established, market\-leading organisation. For this role, you will be required to work on\-site. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and maintain solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI. * Build and customise Canvas Apps and Model\-Driven Apps to support and improve business processes. * Develop automated workflows and integrations using Power Automate. * Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into functional and technical solutions. * Integrate Power Platform solutions with Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft 365, Azure services, and third\-party applications. * Support, maintain, and enhance existing Power Platform applications and workflows. * Design and develop SharePoint Online solutions, including lists, libraries, forms, and collaboration sites. * Ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with best practices and governance standards. * Create reports, dashboards, and business insights using Power BI. * Participate in testing, deployment, and continuous improvement activities across the application lifecycle. * Ensure all solutions comply with security, compliance, and data governance requirements, including GDPR. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline. * Proven commercial experience developing solutions using Microsoft Power Platform. * Strong experience with Power Apps (Canvas and Model\-Driven Apps), Power Automate, and Power BI. * Experience integrating Power Platform solutions with Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft 365, and external data sources. * Knowledge of Dataverse and data modelling principles. * Experience with Azure services and API integrations. * Understanding of solution lifecycle management (ALM) and deployment processes. * Experience using Azure DevOps or similar project management and CI/CD tools. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills. * Knowledge of software development lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies and best practices. * Familiarity with database technologies and data integration techniques. * Relevant Microsoft certifications desirable (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Power Platform Developer Associate, Power Platform Functional Consultant, Dynamics 365 certifications). **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match the requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in more detail. **Similar Roles Available** As a specialist IT recruitment agency, we also have a number of permanent and contract opportunities available across software development, cloud, infrastructure, data, and business applications. Visit our website to explore additional opportunities.

title: Private Equity Fund Accounting Supervisor
description: A leading global fund services provider is continuing to expand its Private Equity Fund Accounting team in Dublin on the back of strong new business growth. This is a hands\-on fund accounting role suited to someone with around 4\+ years’ experience who is ready to step into, or continue developing at, Supervisor level. The position would suit someone who is already comfortable preparing and reviewing NAVs, taking ownership of their own work, and beginning to support others through review, training and day\-to\-day guidance. You will work across a portfolio of private equity, fund of funds and related alternative fund structures, supporting the delivery of accurate and timely NAVs while gaining exposure to a growing book of new business. **The role will involve:** * Preparing and reviewing NAVs for private equity and alternative fund structures * Reviewing capital activity, including capital calls, distributions, drawdowns and investor allocations * Supporting management fee, performance fee and waterfall calculations * Completing and reviewing cash, position and portfolio reconciliations * Liaising with clients, auditors, investment managers and internal teams * Taking ownership of allocated deliverables and ensuring deadlines are met * Reviewing work prepared by junior team members where required * Supporting new joiners through training, guidance and day\-to\-day knowledge sharing * Playing a hands\-on role in process improvement and new business onboarding **The ideal candidate will have:** * Around 4\+ years’ experience in fund accounting, ideally with exposure to private equity, alternatives or closed\-ended funds * Strong NAV preparation and review experience * Good technical accounting knowledge and attention to detail * The ability to work independently and take ownership of deadlines * Some exposure to reviewing work, training juniors or supporting team members * Strong communication skills and the confidence to liaise with internal and external stakeholders * A positive, hands\-on approach and an interest in developing further at supervisory level This is a strong opportunity for someone looking to build their career in private equity fund accounting with a growing global platform. The role offers hands\-on technical work, exposure to new business, and the chance to develop supervisory experience. For a confidential discussion, please apply or get in touch to learn more.

title: Product Engineer
description: **Product Engineer \- Daily Rate up to €600** * Long term, rolling contract * Daily rate up to €600 * Flexible, hybrid work setting A global manufacturing company of food processing solutions is on a mission to become more compliant with the Cyber Resilience Act. As an **Product Engineer,** you will play a critical role in ensuring compliance with relevant regulations and legislation while owning the development of Embedded Linux systems and Azure IoT Edge devices. This is an exceptional opportunity for someone who is hungry for a new challenge and eager to join a Top\-tier team of experts. **Why You'll Love It Here:** * Daily rate €550\-€600 * Opportunity for immediate impact cybersecurity strategy and compliance * Collaborative international engineering environment * Autonomy and ownership in development of a custom Linux based operating system * Long\-term contract opportunity with strong extension potential **You Will:** * Lead the evolution of automated build and release processes, helping deliver secure IoT solutions faster * Implement security controls including secure boot, encryption and certificate management, showing immediate impact * Join an entrepreneurial environment **Skills / Experience:** * Experience with Linux * Cyber Security Experience * Azure IoT For more information, contact nadja.voglar@archer.ie For additional opportunities, visit the Archer Recruitment website

title: Product Specialist – SME Third‑Party & Partnership Operations
description: **Our Purpose** *Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.* **Title And Summary** Product Specialist – SME Third‑Party \& Partnership Operations Why this role matters Small and medium‑sized businesses are the engine of economic growth—and strong partnerships are what enable them to scale. In this role, you’ll sit at the center of a fast‑growing SME ecosystem, helping innovative partners move from idea to market with confidence. As a Product Specialist, you’ll transform complex onboarding, risk, and operational requirements into clear, predictable journeys that unlock growth for partners and the business alike. Your work directly influences speed to market, portfolio health, and partner trust—making this a highly visible, high‑impact role with meaningful room to grow. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives in execution‑heavy environments, enjoys working cross‑functionally, and wants to build deep expertise at the intersection of products, partnerships, and risk. What you’ll be doing Own end‑to‑end onboarding execution for SME ecosystem partners, service providers, and vendors—from initial intake through launch readiness Coordinate risk and due diligence activities, including documentation collection, alignment across teams, and submission readiness Act as the operational quarterback, tracking progress, risks, dependencies, and timelines across multiple partners simultaneously Partner closely with internal stakeholders and external counterparts to maintain momentum and resolve blockers quickly Build and maintain high‑quality onboarding artifacts, including trackers, dashboards, and standardized SME documentation Support remediation and issue resolution, driving follow‑ups, evidence tracking, and clear closure of actions Apply standard onboarding frameworks while adapting to different SME business models, use cases, and partner profiles What we’re looking for Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience Experience in product operations, partner operations, vendor management, compliance, sourcing, or a related field Proven ability to manage multiple workstreams with strong organization and follow‑through High attention to detail paired with clear, confident stakeholder communication A genuine interest in SMEs, fintech, platform products, or partnership‑driven ecosystems What success looks like You independently manage assigned SME partners with confidence and credibility Risks, issues, and dependencies are identified early and handled proactively Internal teams and external partners trust you to keep onboarding moving and aligned You help build scalable, repeatable onboarding outcomes that support portfolio growth You are seen as a go‑to operator and a strong candidate for expanded scope over time **Corporate Security Responsibility** All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: * Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; * Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; * Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and * Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Product Tasters & Foodservice Contacts - German Potato Specialities Ireland
description: We are introducing selected German potato specialities to Ireland under Simple Potatoes \- including GravyCuts and German Potato Pancakes. This is not a typical job. We are looking for a small group of food lovers, pub owners, chefs, kitchen managers, caterers and hospitality contacts in / near Dublin who would like to taste our products and give honest feedback. Our products are designed for Irish pubs, hotels, restaurants, cafés, caterers and selected retail partners. Who we are looking for: • Food lovers who enjoy trying new products • Pub owners, chefs or kitchen managers • Hospitality and foodservice contacts • People interested in German potato specialities • Anyone curious about new food concepts for Ireland What you get: • Free tasting samples • Early access to new German potato products in Ireland • Possibility to introduce the products in your pub, kitchen or business • Direct contact with the founder About Simple Potatoes: Simple Potatoes brings selected German potato specialities from Thuringia to Ireland. Home of GravyCuts and German Potato Pancakes. GravyCuts are German potato dumpling fries \- crispy outside, soft inside, and made for gravy, sauces and loaded dishes. German Potato Pancakes are authentic frozen German potato pancakes for hospitality, foodservice and selected retail. More information: www.simplepotatoes.com⁠ www.gravycuts.com⁠ www.germanpotatopancakes.com⁠ If this sounds interesting, feel free to get in touch. Hagen Founder – Gravy Cuts Food Trading t/a Simple Potatoes

title: Project Engineer
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. **Summary Of Role** Responsible for project engineering aspects within PCI Pharma Services including: * Specification, development, purchase and introduction of site facility, and production equipment tooling and processes in line with the companies and customers’ needs * Liaise with other departments and suppliers during the specification of new equipment to ensure fit for purpose is achieved. * Identification of future Capital investment projects to improve the business offerings and competitive edge * Raising of site capital application requests * To ensure that all engineering \& technical projects are introduced successfully into the business, on time and in budget. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Specification, development and purchase of site facility, equipment and tooling needs. * To ensure that all engineering \& technical projects are introduced successfully into the business, on time and in budget. * Coordination and execution of Factory acceptance (FAT) and Site acceptance (SAT) trials. * Ensure the setup of all technical documentation and paperwork such as tooling logs, setting sheets, on line working sheets, planned preventative maintenance schedules, schedules covering all new equipment and plant brought onto site on the technical services department. * Ensure all activities undertaken are in accordance with statutory, regulatory and company EHS, cGMP and technical standards, procedures and systems. * Ensuring that projects are achieved within the agreed timescale, cost and quality. * Maintenance of the technical operations and project engineering ways of working including but not limited to databases, specifications, templates, SOP’s and training. * Maintenance of the site tooling capability databases and specifications. * Maintenance of the Technical Drawing database. * Producing Technical Drawings \& Templates to support business \& customer needs this includes but is not limited to Tooling, Facility and Engineering drawings within scope of the job\-holder’s capabilities. * In conjunction with the operational group participate in Continuous Improvement Projects as required, including all OEE projects to ensure improved efficiencies, reduced waste and cost. * In conjunction the operational group assist in technical, product and process reviews to resolve operational, performance and functional issues. * Drive a Right First\-Time culture within operations to deliver a Zero\-Defect performance. * Deliver products of Value at Optimal Cost. * Monitoring future technologies and market trends identifying areas for potential investment including reporting and recommendations to senior and executive members of the PCI Pharma Services group. * Build, develop and sustain the knowledge, capability and competency with the Technical Operations team to support ongoing and changing technologies employed to meet the business objectives. * Create and sustain a working environment that promotes the PCI Pharma Services Values and Behaviours. * Connect across PCI Pharma Services to drive performance. * To undertake such tasks and to manage specific or ad hoc projects as and when required to meet department and business needs and within the scope of the job holder’s capabilities. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company. **Essential** **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience** * Engineering degree (Level 7\)/ Trades qualifications/ Other suitable qualification * Two years’ experience working in an Engineering role. * Capability to set clear goals, communicate these clearly and ensure progress to completion meeting predefined targets. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and set priorities. * Problem Solving and Troubleshooting skills. The successful candidate should display a dynamic and proactive approach to manage problems. * Self\-motivated with a results driven approach. * Flexibility to work across different shifts on request in line with business needs. * Adaptable and ability to work collaboratively. * Proficient in Microsoft Office products (incl. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Previous office and operational based project engineering \& packaging experience in a Pharmaceutical or FMCG business * Detailed understanding of facility and engineering issues and processes. * A flexible and willing attitude * Communication skills to interact with internal and external customers at a senior level. * Strong Team player with open communication style with the ability to direct and train others and deliver clear instruction, including internal staff, suppliers, and tradesmen. * Design and develop new equipment to help improve line effectiveness. * Ability to influence and collaborate at all levels in order to achieve company targets. * Ability to represent the company in a professional and highly competent manner. * Ability to multitask and prioritise activities. * Ability to work on their own as well as part of a team. * Excellent problem\-solving skills. * Dependable, reliable and shows good integrity. * Ability to travel on company business as required. **Desirable** * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. * Good working knowledge of cGMP, 21CFR Part11, and GAMP “Current”. * Detailed understanding of blister pack tooling design and manufacturing processes. * Use of creating/amending PLC programs. Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. Let’s talk future **Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Statement** *PCI Pharma Services is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We do not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, marital or domestic or civil union status, familial status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, disability, military eligibility or veteran status, or any other protected status.* *At PCI, Equity and Inclusion are at the core of our company’s purpose: Together, delivering life\-changing therapies. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive workplace by holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of understanding, fairness, respect, and equal opportunity – at every level. We envision a PCI community where everyone can belong and grow, and we strive to bring this vision to reality by continuously and intentionally assessing our people practices, policies and programs, marketing approach, and workplace culture.*

title: Project Finance Lawyer (Energy & Climate)
description: Our client is a leading, full\-service commercial law firm operating at the forefront of the Irish legal market. Renowned for its entrepreneurial culture and market\-leading position in clean energy, sustainability, and green infrastructure, the firm advises an enviable roster of international lenders, specialist non\-bank funders, semi\-states, and premier developers. Due to a substantial pipeline of landmark mandates in the renewable energy, carbon project development, and sustainable infrastructure sectors, they are now seeking a high\-calibre **Senior Associate (4\-8 PQE)** to join their specialist Project Finance practice in Dublin. **The Role** This is a prominent senior position within a collaborative, tier\-ranked team. You will lead on sophisticated transactions shaping the energy transition across Ireland, the UK, and globally. **Key Responsibilities:** * Advising lenders, sponsors, and institutional investors on the structuring, drafting, and negotiation of complex project finance documentation for utility\-scale energy assets (including onshore/offshore wind, solar PV, battery storage, and biomethane). * Drafting and advising on multi\-sourced financing agreements, facility agreements, security packages, and intercreditor arrangements. * Interfacing closely with the firm's dedicated Climate and Environment groups to review and advise on project contracts, corporate Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), and regulatory compliance frameworks. * Taking an active role in transaction management, client relationship development, and the mentorship of junior associates and trainees. **The Candidate** * **Experience:** A qualified solicitor with 4 to 8 years’ PQE specialising in Banking \& Finance, with a clear emphasis on project finance transactions. * **Sector Knowledge:** Proven exposure to the energy, renewables, or climate projects sector is highly desirable. Candidates with strong core banking/finance backgrounds who have a genuine technical interest in transitioning heavily into the energy sector will also be considered. * **Skills:** Exceptional drafting and transaction management capabilities, a commercial mindset, and the confidence to lead client negotiations on high\-value finance structures. * **Culture Fit:** A collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic, progressive environment that prioritizes practical, solution\-focused legal advice. **What the Firm Offers** * **Top\-Tier Work:** Exposure to some of the most innovative and novel green finance and carbon reduction projects in the market today. * **Autonomy \& Progression:** A transparent path to Partnership with formal associate and partner development programmes. * **Modern Working Environment:** A genuinely friendly, inclusive culture that respects individuality and promotes a highly functional hybrid working model. * **Perks \& Wellness:** Extensive CSR initiatives, active green/diversity committees, health/wellness subsidies, and a market\-leading remuneration and bonus structure. For a discreet conversation about the role, please contact Caitriona Breaw at Amicus Search \& Recruitment.

title: Public Sector - Construction Project Delivery Liaison - Major Capital Programme
description: ***Your New Organisation*** Your new organisation is a government\-established agency that leads the transformation of a major inner\-city site into a vibrant, mixed\-use urban quarter. The development integrates a modern university campus, healthcare services, and a wide range of community, cultural, and recreational facilities, creating a dynamic environment for education, health, and community life. ***Your New Role*** The Project Delivery Liaison will play a key role in supporting programme and project delivery, ensuring high\-quality design and construction outcomes across multiple projects.Working collaboratively with internal teams and external stakeholders, the successful candidate will support the coordination, procurement, and delivery of building projects within a structured public capital programme environment.The role includes responsibility for procurement processes relating to technical consultancy services, including preparation of project briefs and associated documentation.The role holder will also contribute to key programme themes such as sustainability, programme delivery, quality management, strategic planning, public procurement, and BIM compliance. Selection of responsibilities: * Promoting excellence in design and construction delivery * Supporting project planning, scheduling, and workflow management in line with national capital delivery frameworks * Managing procurement processes in accordance with public procurement regulations * Assisting in the planning and execution of project tasks to ensure delivery on time, within budget, and to required quality standards * Ensuring compliance with Health \& Safety legislation ***What You'll Need to Succeed*** You should demonstrate: * A recognised third\-level or professional qualification in a relevant discipline (e.g., Architecture, Engineering, Quantity Surveying or similar) * Minimum 5 years' relevant postgraduate experience * Membership (or eligibility) of a recognised professional body * Proven experience delivering construction projects to a high standard, on time and within budget * Strong communication, presentation, and reporting skills * Ability to work independently and collaboratively ***What You'll Get in Return*** * ***€83,000 salary** on a flagship, nationally impactful capital programme* * ***30 days annual leave, significantly above the market,** giving you real, protected downtime* * ***Hybrid working** (1\-2 days per week) with genuine flexibility* * *A role offering the best of both worlds: meaningful, high\-profile work without private\-sector burnout* * *The opportunity to deliver a project focused on investment in education infrastructure across Ireland* * *Work on complex, high\-value projects that will strengthen and elevate your experience* * *A supportive, collaborative environment with experienced professionals and strong governance structures* * *Long\-term career stability and progression within a highly respected public body* **What You Need to do Now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.
